schema_note: FORGE Cycle-003 Sprint 02 (T2.1). Jointly-owned canonicalization parity vectors for jcs-subset/v0. NOT a code module and NOT a behavior change — this file DESCRIBES the existing src/receipt/canonicalize.js behavior so a porter (e.g. Echelon's Python OSINT substrate) and FORGE produce byte-identical canonical output for the same JSON value. The canonicalizer is unchanged this sprint; these vectors pin its behavior.
ownership: Jointly owned by FORGE (producer) and Echelon (receiver). Edits to canonical/reject expectations are a cross-org break and require both sides to re-baseline.
not_full_rfc8785: This is the jcs-subset/v0 narrowing of RFC 8785 — it covers only the JSON value types FORGE shapes contain (object, array, string, finite number, boolean, null) and fail-closes on the rest. It is NOT a full RFC 8785 implementation and these vectors do NOT mandate a switch to one (COMPAT-5: the subset is kept).

parity_basis.bundle_digest: FORGE computes bundle-receipt.json::bundle_digest as sha256(canonicalize(members[])) using this jcs-subset/v0 (src/bundle/receipt.js). These vectors pin that canonicalization so the BREATH bundle_digest is reproducible byte-for-byte across runs and implementations.
parity_basis.cert_hash: Echelon recomputes cert_hash over the ProposalEnvelope MINUS forge_seam.calibration_ref using this SAME jcs-subset/v0. These vectors are the shared parity basis so FORGE's bundle_digest and Echelon's cert_hash cannot diverge across the seam. The cert_hash recompute itself is Echelon-side; FORGE asserts only the canonicalization parity basis (no cert is issued or verified here).

subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.behavior: Emitted via ECMA-262 Number::toString (what V8 JSON.stringify produces). For any input BOTH specs accept, jcs-subset/v0 and RFC 8785 are byte-identical. The subset additionally REJECTS Infinity/-Infinity/NaN (RFC 8785 leaves these to the upstream input contract).
subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.porting_note: Python json.dumps does NOT match ES6 NumberToString on -0.0, on large integers near 10^21, and on small numbers near 10^-6. A correct port reimplements the ECMA-262 algorithm or uses a vetted dtoa library.
Diverges from Python json.dumps(-0.0) which emits "-0.0".

subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.behavior: Emitted via ECMA-262 Quote (what V8 JSON.stringify produces). Control chars use lowercase \uXXXX; non-ASCII (>= U+007F) is emitted as RAW UTF-8 bytes, NOT \u-escaped.
subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.porting_note: Python json.dumps defaults to ensure_ascii=True (escapes non-ASCII). Port MUST use ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',', ':').

subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.behavior: Object keys sorted by UTF-16 code unit order (Array.prototype.sort with no comparator). Same as RFC 8785 §3.2.3 for all-BMP keys.
subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.porting_note: UTF-8-native languages must re-encode keys to UTF-16 and sort by code unit to match for non-BMP keys.

subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.behavior: undefined (top-level / array element), BigInt, Date objects, functions, and symbols THROW. RFC 8785 leaves these to the upstream input contract; jcs-subset/v0 makes the rejection an explicit safety net.
subset_vs_rfc8785_deltas.porting_note: Porters whose value system has no such type need not handle it (the case cannot arise). undefined as an OBJECT PROPERTY VALUE is the one exception — it is silently SKIPPED, mirroring JSON.stringify.

builder_tokens.note: JSON cannot literally hold the values below (no Infinity/NaN/undefined/BigInt/Date/function/symbol, and JSON.parse may normalize -0). Vectors needing such a value carry a `build` token instead of an `input`; the loader (test/unit/jcs-parity.spec.js) maps the token to the JS reference value. These tokens are JS-reference-specific; a porter applies their language's equivalent.
builder_tokens.object_with_undefined_value: { a: 1, b: undefined, c: 3 } — exercises the object-property skip
builder_tokens.object_with_infinity: { value: Infinity } — nested non-finite, must fail closed through object recursion
builder_tokens.array_with_nan: [NaN] — nested NaN, must fail closed through array recursion

reject_vectors.name: reject_infinity
reject_vectors.reason: Infinity is not valid JSON (RFC 8259); rejected fail-closed.

reject_vectors.name: reject_negative_infinity
reject_vectors.reason: -Infinity is not valid JSON; rejected fail-closed.

reject_vectors.name: reject_nan
reject_vectors.reason: NaN is not valid JSON; rejected fail-closed.

reject_vectors.name: reject_undefined_toplevel
reject_vectors.reason: undefined is not a JSON type (top-level / array element).

reject_vectors.name: reject_bigint
reject_vectors.reason: BigInt has no standardized JSON representation.

reject_vectors.name: reject_date
reject_vectors.reason: Date objects must be converted to an ISO-8601 string before canonicalization.

reject_vectors.name: reject_object_with_nested_infinity
reject_vectors.reason: A nested non-finite number fails closed through object recursion (the whole value is rejected, never partially emitted).

reject_vectors.name: reject_array_with_nested_nan
reject_vectors.reason: A nested NaN fails closed through array recursion.
